mentions.note: Caller asked about the recent pullback; Cramer said the weakness is wrong and today's rally confirms Shopify remains a buy.
Down 26% Q1; great business with growth/profitability but 64x earnings unjustifiable; no AI disruption case but money managers won't pay multiple for high-growth SaaS anymore.

AI displacement fears overblown; revenue growth sustained 30%+ for 3 quarters, technician Larry Williams projects seasonal rally Apr–Aug with 70% historical probability, stock already bounced off cycle low.
Universal fulfillment enabler for SMBs; stock meandering but expected good numbers. Must monitor when it reports Tuesday.
34% Q revenue growth, guided high-20s future; stock $182→$127→$105 post-earnings; fabulous company for SMB/Gen-Z; now de-risked; buy when ice-cold.

Beaten down by AI displacement fears but fundamentals excellent (revenue +34%, operating income +88%, FCF +31%). At ~$100 (55x earnings), trades below multi-year valuation bottoms; Cramer calls it a 'steal.'
